rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes
Place implementation annexes in valid component implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use visible identifiers from the current target component or implementation
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are properly scoped within the AADL architecture
Avoid placing assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in invalid scopes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations in component type annexes
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in invalid scopes
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
